title: json_parse_string rejects valid JSON surrogate pairs
description: Trigger: a JSON secret value whose string field is encoded with a \uXXXX surrogate pair, e.g. {"password":"\uD83D\uDE00secret"} (emoji via UTF-16 surrogate pair, which is valid per RFC 8259 section 7). Bad behavior: char::from_u32(0xD800..0xDFFF) returns None, so extract_field returns Err(DecodeFailed) instead of the decoded value. This is a regression from the old serde_json path, which handled surrogate pairs correctly. Real-world trigger: any AWS SM or Vault secret containing emojis or other supplementary Unicode stored by a Windows-origin JSON serializer (e.g. .NET's JsonSerializer) that uses surrogate pairs. Fix: after reading a \uXXXX code, check if it is in the high-surrogate range (0xD800–0xDBFF); if so, expect a \uDC00–\uDFFF low-surrogate follow-up and combine to U+10000+. Return DecodeFailed for lone surrogates.
